The following table describes response headers that are common to most Amazon S3 responses. ntrol-All ow-Creden tials A Boolean that determines if the server allows CORS requests to contain credentials. If the Access-Control-Allow-Origin request header is set to '*' then the Access-Control-Allow-Credentials response header will be omitted, else it is set to true when CORS evaluatio n is successful. ntrol-All ow-Headers A list of HTTP headers allowed for your CORS requests. The Access-Co ntrol-Allow-Headers response header is returned for successful CORS evaluations and explicitly specifies all allowed Access-Control- Request-Headers. ntrol-All ow-Methods A list that specifies which HTTP methods are allowed. Amazon S3 will only allow CORS requests from allowed CORS methods when the CORS evaluation is successful. ntrol-All ow-Origin The location of the allowed origin. Amazon S3 will only send the Access- Control-Allow-Origin response header when the CORS evaluation is successful. If the request origin matches '*' in the CORS configuration's allowed origins then the '*' is returned in this response header instead of the original origin. ntrol-Exp ose-Headers A list that allows a server to identify a response header that exposes access for applications when the CORS evaluation is successful. The Access-Control-Max-Age response header is only returned when the CORS evaluation is successful. Vary A list that indicates which request headers the CORS evaluation result varies on.
